projectNeed: prompt-heavy PoC must be converted into code-owned, measurable LLM program boundaries
answer composer must be optimized against frozen advisor scenarios and source-backed trace checks
framework must fit LLM Wiki, RAG, source-backed claims, and autoresearch-style evaluation
selectionBasis: declarative signatures and modules fit the advisor composer boundary
optimizers can tune instructions, few-shot examples, or weights against project metrics
RAG and agent-loop examples map well to the public-data investor advisor architecture
clear research citation path and active ecosystem

notSelectedFirst.reason: Good for constrained decoding and control flow, but less central than metric-driven module optimization for this paper.
Excellent for typed extraction; too narrow as the primary framework because it does not optimize multi-stage LM programs.
Strong generation control, but the project currently needs scenario-level optimization more than token-level constraints.
Strong optimization research reference, but more experimental for this production-preparation pipeline.
Relevant workflow optimization library, but DSPy has a clearer fit with signatures, RAG modules, and established citation path.
Useful later for cost and speed after enough production traces exist; not the first framework for a pre-API reference PoC.

adoptionPlan: Keep Node/Replit runtime lightweight while trace and source contracts stabilize.
Add DSPy-compatible composer boundary to documentation and trace terminology.
Keep the live LLM runtime boundary schema-validated before adopting any optimized composer.
Create an offline Python sidecar experiment after the Hanwha paper baseline is frozen.
Use advisor-answer-quality-v0.2 as the metric for composer optimization.
Adopt optimized composer in runtime only if it beats deterministic baseline without development leakage.
runtimeDecision: Do not install DSPy into the production/demo runtime yet. Treat DSPy as the selected paper framework and next offline optimization layer. For the live LLM boundary, use a small code-owned structured-output contract now; Instructor can replace the hand-rolled validator later if live structured generation becomes central.
